name: Aiea
desc: Adjacent to Pearl Harbor — Aiea Heights trail, Pearlridge access, working-class residential, convenient but unremarkable.
insider: Aiea Heights Trail is one of the best moderate hikes near Honolulu — panoramic views of Pearl Harbor, the Waianae Range, and Diamond Head on clear days
Champa Thai on Moanalua Road is a local sleeper pick — the green curry and pad see ew are excellent and dirt cheap
Pearlridge Center is the biggest mall outside Waikiki with two buildings connected by Hawaii's only monorail — the Uptown side is quieter for shopping
Pearl Harbor National Memorial is right here — visit on a weekday morning to dodge the bus-tour crowds that arrive by 10am
Buzz's Original Steak House on Kamehameha Highway has been feeding locals since 1967 — order the garlic steak
The Aloha Stadium swap meet on weekends is the island's best flea market for souvenirs, aloha shirts, and cheap coconut water — arrive before 9am
H-1 and H-78 access makes Aiea one of the most commuter-friendly spots on Oahu, but rush hour westbound backs up at the Halawa interchange every evening
The old Aiea Sugar Mill site along the waterfront is being redeveloped — a piece of plantation-era history most people drive right past
Shiro's Saimin Haven on Kamehameha Highway serves some of the best saimin on Oahu — a tiny counter spot locals guard jealously
Break-ins in parked cars near trailheads happen regularly — never leave valuables visible at the Aiea Heights parking lot
